title: Director of Information Technology
raw_jd:
About the job

Overview

The Squires Group is seeking an accomplished Director of Information Technology to lead enterprise‑wide technology strategy and operations within a government‑focused environment. This senior‑level role is responsible for overseeing all business technology functions while ensuring systems are secure, scalable, and aligned with organizational objectives and federal security requirements.

This position is primarily onsite at a secured headquarters location in Fairfax, VA, with limited remote flexibility available following an initial performance period.

Per contract requirements, candidates must be U.S. Citizens with an active Secret Clearance.

Responsibilities

     Lead enterprise IT strategy and operations across infrastructure, cloud platforms, cybersecurity, and compliance 
     Manage cross ‑ functional IT teams and vendor partners 
     Drive organization ‑ wide technology upgrades, modernization, and digital transformation initiatives 
     Partner with business and executive leadership to deliver scalable, mission ‑ aligned technology solutions 
     Oversee risk management, vulnerability assessments, and incident response activities 
     Manage IT budgets, vendor relationships, and cost optimization efforts 
     Ensure operational resilience through disaster recovery and business continuity planning 
     Support user enablement through training and adoption of new technologies 

Qualifications

     10+ years of experience in information technology roles 
     5+ years of IT management and leadership experience 
     Proven experience leading diverse IT teams and cross ‑ functional initiatives 
     Strong hands ‑ on knowledge of enterprise infrastructure, cloud platforms, and security frameworks, including Microsoft Azure, Microsoft 365, and ServiceNow 
     Demonstrated experience supporting federal compliance and security standards, including CMMC 2.0, Risk Management Framework (RMF), ITAR, and classified environments 
     Ability to align technology strategy with business outcomes 
     Experience managing large ‑ scale technology projects 
     Strong background in budget ownership and vendor management 
     Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Security, or a related field 

Preferred Experience

     Background in a government contracting corporate IT environment (internal enterprise IT), rather than contract‑only or customer‑site IT support. 

Per contract requirements, candidates must be
 U.S. Citizens with an active Secret Clearance.

Compensation And Benefits

     Salary range of $200K-$220K, based on experience, qualifications, location, and certifications. The salary pay range is subject to change and may be modified at any time. 
     Benefits package includes PTO, health, vision, and dental coverage, 401K with some company match, Wellness program, FSA, EAP, and more! 

 Check out our Referral Program! 

The Squires Group will pay you for every qualified professional that you refer and we place. If you see a position posted by The Squires Group and know the perfect person for the job, please send us your referral. For more information, go to https://bit.ly/squiresreferral .

Benefits found in job post

401(k)

score.skills_rationale: The role requires traditional IT infrastructure, cloud platforms (Azure, M365), and security frameworks which align partially with my technical background. However, my core expertise in data architecture, analytics, BI platforms, and ERP systems isn't directly relevant to this enterprise IT leadership position. There's some overlap in cloud/Azure experience and general technical leadership.
score.scope_rationale: This is an ideal scope match - it's a senior director role requiring 10+ years experience and 5+ years leadership, which aligns perfectly with my career progression from individual contributor to architect to senior leadership roles. The cross-functional leadership, vendor management, and strategic technology planning responsibilities match my background.
score.pay_rationale: The $200K-$220K salary range is within my W2 target range of $160K-$220K, making this financially attractive.
score.raw_analysis: This is a legitimate senior IT director role with appropriate compensation, but it's focused on traditional enterprise IT infrastructure and security rather than data/analytics. While the seniority level matches perfectly, the skills gap is significant - they need someone with deep cybersecurity, compliance, and traditional IT operations experience. The Secret clearance requirement and Fairfax location also present barriers. This represents a domain switch that doesn't leverage my core data architecture and analytics expertise.

cover_letters.content:
Dear Hiring Manager,

As a senior technology leader with 25+ years of enterprise systems experience and proven success directing complex, multi-million-dollar implementations across government and federal environments, I'm positioned to drive The Squires Group's IT strategy and operations forward. My current role leading analytics architecture for the VA's Financial Management Business Transformation program has strengthened my expertise in federal compliance requirements, cross-functional stakeholder management, and delivering mission-critical systems within secure government frameworks.

My leadership experience spans the full spectrum of what this role demands. At Howard University, I served as one of three cross-functional drivers during a major Workday ERP implementation, working directly with the CFO while coordinating across Finance, HR, and IT teams during a period of executive disruption. This experience mirrors the vendor management and strategic technology planning responsibilities outlined in your requirements. Earlier, as BI Senior Manager at VitalSpring Technologies, I owned P&L responsibility for analytics products while managing technical teams and driving process improvements that delivered $70K+ in cost savings within six months.

While my core expertise centers on data architecture and enterprise reporting systems rather than traditional IT infrastructure, my hands-on experience with Linux environments, Docker, distributed systems, and Azure cloud services provides a strong technical foundation. My CISSP certification demonstrates commitment to security frameworks, and my extensive work with federal systems has given me practical exposure to compliance requirements and secure environments. The cross-functional leadership skills I've developed—from coordinating finance and acquisition stakeholders at the VA to mentoring technical teams across multiple organizations—directly translate to managing diverse IT teams and driving organization-wide technology initiatives.

I'm prepared to leverage my strategic mindset, proven ability to deliver complex technical programs, and deep understanding of government operational requirements to advance your enterprise IT objectives. I look forward to discussing how my leadership experience and technical expertise can contribute to The Squires Group's continued success.

Sincerely,
Salil Maniktahla

Dear Hiring Manager,

As someone who has spent over two decades bridging the gap between complex technical systems and business objectives, I understand the unique challenges The Squires Group faces in maintaining secure, scalable enterprise IT operations within a government contracting environment. Your need for a Director of Information Technology who can drive strategic technology initiatives while ensuring federal compliance resonates with my experience leading cross-functional teams through large-scale system implementations and transformations.

My current role as Senior Data & Analytics Architect supporting the VA's Financial Management Business Transformation program has given me deep insight into the complexities of enterprise IT within federal environments. I've successfully coordinated across finance, acquisition, and IT stakeholders to deliver mission-critical systems while maintaining strict security and audit requirements. Similarly, during Howard University's multi-million-dollar Workday ERP implementation, I served as one of three key cross-functional drivers, leading data governance initiatives and collaborating directly with executive leadership during a period of organizational transition. This experience taught me how to align technology strategy with business outcomes while managing vendor relationships and ensuring delivery under pressure.

While my recent focus has been on data architecture and analytics, my foundation spans traditional enterprise IT leadership. At VitalSpring Technologies, I managed BI/analytics across the organization, identified process bottlenecks that saved over $70K in six months, and led Agile delivery initiatives. My earlier experience includes technical architecture for large-scale enterprise platforms and team leadership roles managing diverse technical teams. Though I would need to refresh my knowledge of current federal compliance frameworks like CMMC 2.0 and RMF, my proven ability to quickly master complex regulatory environments and translate technical requirements into business value positions me to contribute meaningfully to your team's success.

I'm drawn to this opportunity because it combines strategic technology leadership with hands-on problem-solving in an environment where precision and security are paramount. I'd welcome the chance to discuss how my experience leading technology transformations and managing cross-functional stakeholders can support The Squires Group's mission and growth objectives.

Sincerely,
Salil Maniktahla

I am writing to express my interest in the Director of Information Technology position at The Squires Group. With 25+ years of enterprise technology leadership, I bring the strategic vision and hands-on execution this role demands. As Senior Data & Analytics Architect for the VA's FMBT program, I've led cross-functional teams implementing enterprise ERP systems across complex federal environments, coordinating between finance, acquisition, and IT stakeholders to deliver mission-critical solutions. My previous role at Howard University involved driving a multi-million-dollar Workday implementation as one of three key leaders during executive disruption, successfully stabilizing delivery and achieving deployment.

My technical foundation spans enterprise infrastructure, cloud platforms including Azure, and distributed systems architecture, complemented by proven budget ownership and vendor management experience. Having worked extensively in federal environments including the VA and EPA, I understand the compliance rigor and security frameworks essential for government contractors. While I recognize this role emphasizes traditional IT operations over my data architecture specialty, my track record of translating complex technical challenges into business outcomes, leading diverse teams, and delivering enterprise-scale solutions positions me to drive The Squires Group's technology strategy and modernization initiatives effectively.

tailored_resume:
SALIL MANIKTAHLA
Springfield, VA  ·  571-215-8218  ·  salil.maniktahla@gmail.com  ·  linkedin.com/in/salilmaniktahla

SUMMARY
IT and data architecture leader with 25+ years delivering enterprise technology operations, infrastructure management, and cross-functional leadership across government and corporate environments. Currently leading technology initiatives for VA's enterprise modernization program with prior experience as CTO/CIO scaling multi-location operations and IT teams.

Proven track record of enterprise IT strategy, vendor management, budget ownership, and large-scale technology project delivery. Strong background in Azure cloud platforms, enterprise infrastructure, and business technology alignment with hands-on technical depth and executive-level stakeholder management.

CORE CAPABILITIES
Enterprise IT Leadership & Strategy
    • Cross-functional IT team management, vendor partnerships, and technology modernization initiatives
    • Budget ownership, cost optimization, and large-scale technology project delivery
    • Enterprise infrastructure, cloud platforms (Azure), and scalable technology solutions

Business Technology Alignment
    • Executive partnership translating business requirements into technology strategy
    • Digital transformation, ERP implementations, and organizational technology adoption
    • Risk management, operational resilience, and business continuity planning

Infrastructure & Operations
    • Linux systems, networking, distributed systems, enterprise security architecture
    • Cloud platforms (Azure data services), disaster recovery, and system integration
    • Performance optimization, monitoring, and incident response capabilities

PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE
Senior Data & Analytics Architect (Contractor)
U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs – FMBT	Sep 2021 – Present
    • Lead technology strategy and operations for Financial Management Business Transformation program, coordinating across finance, IT, and acquisition stakeholders to deliver enterprise modernization
    • Manage vendor relationships and cross-functional delivery teams supporting large-scale ERP rollout across multiple implementation waves
    • Drive technology upgrades and system integration efforts bridging legacy infrastructure and modernized platforms
    • Oversee risk management and data validation processes ensuring operational resilience and audit readiness
    • Partner with executive leadership to align technology solutions with mission objectives and federal requirements

Business Intelligence Analyst – Finance
Howard University	Apr 2020 – Jul 2024
    • Served as key technology leader in multi-million-dollar Workday ERP implementation, managing cross-functional initiatives during executive disruption (CIO departure)

    • Drove organizational technology adoption through training and change management across multiple departments
    • Owned data governance strategy and technology policy implementation across financial and operational systems

Co-Founder / CEO & Technical Director
Urban Evolution	Apr 2010 – Apr 2020
    • Served as CTO and CIO, owning all technology strategy, IT infrastructure, and operations across multi-location franchise business
    • Built and managed enterprise IT infrastructure, networking, and distributed systems supporting business operations
    • Led technology team hiring, training, and development while maintaining hands-on technical involvement
    • Managed IT budgets, vendor relationships, and cost optimization across corporate and franchise locations

BI Partner / Strategist (Independent Consultant)
Multiple Clients	Jul 2016 – Mar 2020
    • Delivered IT strategy and technology architecture for enterprise clients, managing large-scale technology deployments
    • Led cross-functional technology initiatives supporting 350+ users globally across distributed environments
    • Managed vendor relationships and technology project delivery aligned with governance and compliance requirements
    • Mentored technical teams and supported organizational technology adoption and change management

VitalSpring Technologies
Senior BI Engineer → BI Manager → BI Senior Manager	May 2008 – Jul 2016
BI Senior Manager
    • Led enterprise technology strategy and operations for healthcare analytics platform serving enterprise clients
    • Managed IT teams and vendor partnerships while driving technology modernization initiatives
    • Owned budget management and cost optimization, delivering $70K+ in operational savings through technology improvements
    • Drove large-scale technology projects using Agile methodologies and cross-functional team leadership

EARLIER EXPERIENCE
Senior BI Consultant – Claraview (Teradata), 2006–2008
    • Technology architect for 25,000-user enterprise platform (Lowe's); managed large-scale infrastructure deployment
IT Specialist – U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, 2002–2005
    • Defined IT strategy for National Center for Environmental Economics; led team of 6 developers and engineers

Corporate Network Engineer – CyberRep, 2001–2002
    • Designed corporate network security architecture and enterprise infrastructure systems
Operations Manager – MicroStrategy, 1999–2001
    • Led team of systems engineers; managed enterprise technology operations and vendor optimization saving ~$312K annually
Systems Analyst – Cexec / U.S. State Department, 1998–1999
    • WAN/LAN architecture and enterprise network operations in federal contracting environment
Enterprise Consulting – TekSystems, 1996–1998
    • Led enterprise migration projects and multi-site technology deployments across US/Canada

SELECTED INNOVATION
    • Designed enterprise technology platform combining distributed systems, automation, and monitoring across multiple environments

TECHNICAL SKILLS

CERTIFICATIONS & EDUCATION

CISSP  ·  Certified Scrum Master  ·  Tableau Certified Professional  ·  MicroStrategy Certified Developer
